diagrams.title: Segmentation Concept
diagrams.description: Shows how logical memory is divided into variable-sized segments (code, data, stack, heap) and how a segment table maps these logical segments to physical memory addresses. This helps visualize how segmentation provides logical view of memory with protection and sharing per segment.
elements: Logical Address Space (segments of varying sizes)
Segment Table (segment number, base address, limit)
diagrams.purpose: Explains segmentation as a memory management scheme that divides memory into variable-sized logical units

diagrams.title: Paging Concept
diagrams.description: Illustrates how logical memory is divided into fixed-size pages and physical memory into frames of the same size. Shows the page table mapping process and how virtual addresses are translated to physical addresses using page number and offset.
diagrams.purpose: Explains paging as a memory management scheme that eliminates external fragmentation by using fixed-size pages

diagrams.title: Combined Segmentation and Paging
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the two-level address translation: first the segment selector identifies the segment, then the page within that segment is mapped to a physical frame. Shows how segmentation provides protection and paging provides efficient memory allocation.
elements: Segment Table Entry (points to page table)
Page Table (page to frame within segment)
diagrams.purpose: Shows how segmentation and paging are combined to get benefits of both: logical protection and efficient physical memory use
